name: SnapLogic Intelligent Integration Platform API
what_it_does: SnapLogic Intelligent Integration Platform REST API for iPaaS and data integration automation. Enables AI agents to manage pipeline creation and configuration via API, handle pipeline execution and scheduling, access Snap (connector) catalog management for 700+ pre-built integrations, retrieve pipeline run status and error monitoring, manage project and asset organization workflows, handle API gateway and proxy management, access data transformation and mapping configuration, retrieve integration analytics and performance data, manage user and permission provisioning, and integrate SnapLogic pipeline orchestration with enterprise data warehouses, SaaS apps, and cloud platforms.
use_cases: Triggering and monitoring integration pipelines from enterprise automation agents
Managing pipeline deployment and configuration from DevOps agents
Accessing integration analytics and error reporting from monitoring agents
Orchestrating data pipeline runs from data engineering agents
not_for: Simple single-point API integrations without enterprise iPaaS complexity
Event streaming requiring Kafka-native throughput without iPaaS overhead
Open-source integration without enterprise support requirements
best_when: An enterprise using SnapLogic wants AI agents to automate pipeline triggering, deployment management, error monitoring, and integration orchestration across cloud and on-premises systems.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated pipeline deployment can modify production data integrations — staging/testing environments and deployment gates are essential. Pipeline error handling automation must distinguish transient vs persistent failures to avoid retry storms.

auth.notes: SnapLogic uses Bearer token authentication. API tokens generated from user account. REST API for pipeline management and execution. Comprehensive Atlassian-hosted documentation. No native webhook support — polling recommended for pipeline status. SnapLogic Iris AI for intelligent pipeline suggestions.

pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription; pricing based on integration nodes and data volume
pricing.notes: San Mateo, California. Founded 2006. Enterprise iPaaS platform. Private, backed by Andreessen Horowitz. 700+ enterprise customers including Adobe and Cisco. 700+ pre-built Snaps (connectors). GenAI integration with SnapLogic Iris for AI-assisted pipeline building. Competes with MuleSoft and Boomi for enterprise iPaaS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API token; pipeline execution throttled

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Pipeline deployment automation can overwrite production integrations — use project namespacing and staging environments
API token scope — single account-level token without granular scopes; protect carefully
No public MCP server — REST API via Atlassian documentation portal
No native webhooks — poll pipeline run status; implement backoff to avoid rate limiting
Enterprise licensing required — no self-serve API access for production deployments
SnapLogic Iris AI — AI-assisted pipeline building available but requires human review before production deployment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise integration platform. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Bearer token. Multi-region. Pipeline configuration and data flow security.

security_score_components.security_notes: iPaaS and data integration. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Bearer token. Multi-region. Pipeline and integration data.
